ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of The Real Property
Off Calendar Oct-23-2024 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Aug-26-2024 Continued To Oct-23-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Listing Of The House
Off Calendar Aug-26-2024 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order To Sell The Trust Property "As-Is"
Off Calendar Aug-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Etan Rosen appeared by zoom for and with Jeri Rowland by zoom.
Konstantine Demiris appeared by zoom for and with Paul Lucero (co-trustee) by zoom.

Mr. Rosen stated that it is his understanding that the house is ready to be sold, that he wants an order for the house to be put on the market on August 16th, that he does not want any more delay, that the estate money is going to run out and that he does not want a foreclosure. Mr. Demiris stated that the house is getting listed this month, that he does not think an order is necessary and that all of the parties agree that the house will be sold. The Petition for an Order to Sell the Trust Property is under submission. The court will order that the house be listed on or before August 16, 2024. Status on listing of the house is set for August 26, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re Loan To Complete Remodel Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jan-03-2024 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order To Sell The Trust Property "As-Is"
Off Calendar Jan-03-2024 Continued To Mar-04-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Etan Rosen, Esq., via Zoom, for and with Jeri Rowland, Petitioner.
Konstantine Demiris, Esq., via Zoom, for Paul Lucero, Successor Trustee.

At the outset, counsel offers their updates to the court regarding the status of the loan to remodel the estate property, with Mr. Rosen advising that loan documents are pending. The court acknowledges counsel's reports and after additional discussion, advises that it intends to take the Status RE Loan to Complete Remodel of Real Property off calendar and set a date for Status of Repairs to Real Property. No objection to the court's tentative ruling is presented.

The Status RE Loan, etc., is taken off calendar. The Petition for an Order to Sell the Trust Property "As-is", is continued to Mar-04-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A hearing on Status of Repairs to Real Property is added for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross. C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Petrucci. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions (Petition Filed In Consolidated Case 303954 On 5/3/21)
Off Calendar Dec-06-2023 Text Ruling

Petition For An Order To Sell The Trust Property "As-Is"
Off Calendar Dec-06-2023 Continued To Jan-03-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):
Etan Rosen, Esq., for and with Jeri Rowland, Petitioner.
Konstantine Demiris, Esq., for and with Paul Lucero, Objector.
Kevin Curran, in propria persona, General Contractor.
Jeremy Rushton, in propria persona, Real Estate Agent.
Craig Durkin, in propria persona, Beneficiary.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding their competing petitions. Mr. Curran reports regarding his work with the trust's real property as contractor and that he is still owed money for work that he's performed. Mr. Rushton reports as to how much he believes the real property might sell for "as is", compared to after a remodel. There is discussion regarding the estimated costs to remodel the real property and how the funds to do so would be obtained.

After additional discussion, Mr. Rosen advises that he wishes to withdraw his Petition for Instructions. The court acknowledges the request, and the Petition is withdrawn in open court pursuant to Mr. Rosen's request. The remaining subject matter is continued to Jan-03-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status RE: Obtaining a Loan for the Completion of A Remodel is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions (Petition Filed In Consolidated Case 303954 On 5/3/21)
Off Calendar Jun-26-2023 Continued To Sep-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Etan Rosen, for and with Jeri Rowland, via Zoom, Petitioner; Konstantine Demiris, via Zoom, for Paul Lucero, Successor Trustee.

Messrs. Rosen and Demiris offer a status of the real property and the possible timing of the sale of the same. There is discussion regarding whether the court can order a deadline to complete renovations of the real property. The court offers its position and confirms that it is not ready to set a deadline and invites Mr. Rosen to file a petition if he wishes to expedite the sale of the real property.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Sep-18-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions (Petition Filed In Consolidated Case 303954 On 5/3/21)
Off Calendar Mar-27-2023 Continued To Jun-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Etan Rosen appeared by zoom for and with Jeri Rowland by zoom.

The court stated that it read Mr. Rosen's submission regarding the request for a 120-day continuance. Mr. Rosen stated that he did not speak with Mr. Demiris about the continuance, reported on the property, on how much money has been used from the hard money loan for repairs, on how much money is left, and on other assets that are expected to come in. Mrs. Rowland expressed her concerns, objected to the length of the continuance and suggested a three month continuance. The Petition for Instructions is continued to June 26, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Rosen will advise Mr. Demiris of the hearing date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-23-2021 Continued To Dec-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Samuel Swenson appeared by telephone for Petitioner.
Kenneth McKenzie appeared by telephone for Paul Lucero (Respondent/Trustee).

Mr. McKenzie stated that they completed the accounting for the period of August 2016 through August of this year, that the Arnold property was appraised, that the appraisal was provided to Mr. Swenson on November 4th and that he has not heard from him. Mr. Swenson stated that he received the accounting, that he is going through it with his Client, that they will need to go back and forth with the Trustee before firmly going forward with the breach of trust and that they need 90-days before they can discuss mediation. The Court stated that this is the fifth calling of this petition, that the relief sought was the accounting, that there will be no amending of the petition and that it will be resolved today. The Court also noted that the intent of the Settlor was to stay out of Court, that this case has been in Court for one year now, stated that it is time to resolve the petition and move on, that if the Parties have any other issues, they can file a new petition and that it has no intention to bring this trust under Court supervision. Mr. Swenson stated that he agrees that they now have want they requested in their petition. The Petition for Breach of Trust is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-18-2019 Continued To Dec-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Continued To Nov-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Samuel Swenson appearing by CourtCall for Jeri Rowland. Kenneth B. McKenzie appearing by CourtCall for Paul Lucero.

Mr. McKenzie stated that a CPA was hired and that more time is needed. Mr. Swenson stated that his client is getting impatient. Mr. McKenzie followed up by stating that they sent Mr. Swenson the inventory part of the accounting, that they are getting close, that they should have it completed in several weeks or a month, and that then will address distribution. Mr. Swenson stated that he wanted the accounting on file in case there are objections. Mr. McKenzie stated that once they had an accounting/that the parties had the numbers that they would then put together a distribution. Mr. Swenson stated that there are four heirs from two separate families and that the other two heirs are not represented by counsel. Mr. Swenson confirmed for the Court that there would not be equal shares to all four heirs. Mr. McKenzie stated that three of the four heirs will receive equal distribution after a $50,000.00 distribution is made to one heir and that they are talking about a couple million dollars in total. Mr. McKenzie also stated that there is a property in SF that is to be sold. Mr. Swenson stated that his client is aging and that they want the administration done quickly. Mr. McKenzie stated that they are working on the kitchen, that it should be done in two to three weeks, and that they should be able to get the property on the market soon. Mr. Swenson stated that there is a cabin in Arnold that the Trustee intends to keep and that it needs an appraisal. The Court stated that the parties should get to a meeting of the minds regarding the appraisal process. Mr. McKenzie stated that they haven't gotten an appraisal yet, that they will get one, that then they can get a second appraisal of their chosing, and that there is no dispute with regard to distribution just the accounting. Mr. McKenzie addressed Jeri Rowland putting the property in her name. Mr. Swenson stated that it was a misunderstanding which has been cleared up. Mr. Swenson addressed the tax issues stating that his client paid out of pocket. The Court stated that information should be sent to Mr. McKenzie for full disclosure. Mr. McKenzie stated that they had difficulty obtaining bank records, that they weren't in the name of the Trust, and that they would have them or early bank records in the name of the settlors. Mr. Swenson addressed attorney's fees and the possibility of the need for another hearing. Mr. McKenzie stated that the issue was to remove his client, that he asked that the petition go off calendar, and then inquired about what the attorney's fees would be for at this time. The Court stated that the attorney's fees are not before it today, encouraged the parties to keep the communication open, and then continued the Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust to November 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status and to see if the parties are still on track.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Kenneth McKenzie appeared by telephone for Paul Lucero (Respondent).

Mr. Swenson stated that he got a preliminary accounting from the Trustee and requested a
60-day continuance for the Trustee to provide a formal accounting. Mr. McKenzie stated that he is fine with it, but 90-days seem more practical and that he hopes they will agree to a formal settlement. The Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust is continued to September 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the accounting and status of the settlement agreement.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Continued To Jun-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Samuel Swenson appeared by telephone for Jeri Rowland (Petitioner).
Kenneth McKenzie appeared by telephone for Paul Lucero (Respondent).

Mr. Swenson stated that they had a settlement agreement to provide an accounting, that the Grantor has passed away, requested 60-days to allow opposing Counsel to put together an accounting, stated that the Co-Trustees will work together and that he would like the Court to still retain jurisdiction over the trust. Mr. McKenzie stated that he does not believe there is anything substantial in the estate. The Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust is continued to June 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Continued To Feb-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Samuel Swenson for and with Jeri Rowland.
Ken McKenzie for Paul Lucero (Trustee/Respondent).

Mr. McKenzie stated that he and Counsel stipulated to his Client providing an accounting within six weeks, informed the Court that Elsie (sole surviving settlor) is very ill and has a matter of weeks to live, stated that at that point the issue of who the Trustee is will be moot, that they are actively working to do an accounting and that he does not see why the petition could not be denied. Mr. Swenson stated that it was an ambiguous trust, that it was poorly drafted, that he prefers to have the matter continued and that if the matter is going to be denied it should be done on the merits. Mr. Swenson also stated that all his Client wants is an accounting and that his Client was not being provided with any information. The Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust is continued to February 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
